name: Simple ORM with In-Memory Storage
description: Implement a tiny ORM-like framework backed by an in-memory store across two modules. Module orm_fields.py must define: Field base class with constructor Field(default=None, required=False, validator=None), method validate(value) raising ValueError if invalid; subclasses IntField, StringField, BoolField that enforce type checking; Model metaclass-driven base class so that subclasses declare class-level Field attributes which become instance attributes after construction. Module orm_store.py must import from orm_fields.py and implement: Store class with methods register(model_cls), insert(instance) returns assigned id, get(model_cls, id), all(model_cls) returns list, filter(model_cls, **kwargs) returns matching instances, delete(model_cls, id) returns True if removed, count(model_cls) returns int. Each instance gets an auto-incrementing id unique per model class on insert.
test_imports: from orm_fields import Field, IntField, StringField, BoolField, Model
signature:
# orm_fields.py
class Field:
    def __init__(self, default=None, required=False, validator=None): ...
    def validate(self, value): ...

class IntField(Field): ...
class StringField(Field): ...
class BoolField(Field): ...

class Model:
    def __init__(self, **kwargs): ...

# orm_store.py
from orm_fields import Model

class Store:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def register(self, model_cls) -> None: ...
    def insert(self, instance) -> int: ...
    def get(self, model_cls, id): ...
    def all(self, model_cls) -> list: ...
    def filter(self, model_cls, **kwargs) -> list: ...
    def delete(self, model_cls, id) -> bool: ...
    def count(self, model_cls) -> int: ...
tests:
def test_store_insert_get():
    class U(Model):
        name = StringField()
    s = Store()
    s.register(U)
    uid = s.insert(U(name='Alice'))
    fetched = s.get(U, uid)
    assert fetched.name == 'Alice'

def test_store_auto_id():
    class U(Model):
        name = StringField()
    s = Store()
    s.register(U)
    a = s.insert(U(name='A'))
    b = s.insert(U(name='B'))
    assert a != b

def test_store_filter():
    class U(Model):
        name = StringField()
        active = BoolField(default=True)
    s = Store()
    s.register(U)
    s.insert(U(name='A', active=True))
    s.insert(U(name='B', active=False))
    s.insert(U(name='C', active=True))
    actives = s.filter(U, active=True)
    assert len(actives) == 2

def test_store_delete():
    class U(Model):
        name = StringField()
    s = Store()
    s.register(U)
    uid = s.insert(U(name='X'))
    assert s.delete(U, uid) is True
    assert s.get(U, uid) is None
    assert s.delete(U, uid) is False

def test_store_count():
    class U(Model):
        name = StringField()
    s = Store()
    s.register(U)
    s.insert(U(name='A'))
    s.insert(U(name='B'))
    assert s.count(U) == 2
constraints: Field type checks must raise ValueError, not TypeError
Model class attributes that are Field instances become instance attributes after __init__
Each model_cls has its own id counter starting at 1
get() returns None if id not found, does not raise
filter() supports equality matching on any registered field
Required fields without default must be provided as kwargs
